title: Dyspnea and Dullness - Pleural Effusion
chief_complaint: Progressive shortness of breath for 2 weeks, right-sided chest heaviness, dry cough. Weight loss of 5 kg in 2 months.

physical_exam.chest: Decreased breath sounds right base, dullness to percussion, decreased vocal fremitus, trachea shifted left

hidden_data.cxr: Large right pleural effusion with contralateral mediastinal shift
hidden_data.thoracentesis: Exudative: Protein 5.2 g/dL (serum 7.0), LDH 350 (serum 220), cytology positive for adenocarcinoma

key_learning_points: Exudate = protein ratio >0.5 or LDH ratio >0.6

patient_responses.breathing: I can't lie flat, I need 2 pillows

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Malignant effusion - the cytology is key.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is a malignant pleural effusion. The exudative nature and positive cytology confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Malignant effusion: therapeutic thoracentesis for symptom relief. Consider tunneled pleural catheter for recurrent effusions. Refer to oncology. The smoking history is significant.
